behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is not about wrapper logic; it is about translation quality itself. REAL-INFRA: No OS/subprocess requirement beyond making the model/API call. MOCK-HIDE: A stubbed translator can always return perfect fixtures and hide real quality failures. LLM-DEP: Correctness depends on real model output quality and can vary across inputs/runs. MULTI-STEP: Single model decision, so not Workflow. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: An Integration test with canned translations for idioms (e.g., "kick the bucket") passes, while the real model returns a literal phrase that loses meaning. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text describes one translation step, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus-based quality test against the real translation model/API (no output stubbing), scoring semantic preservation and fluency (e.g., human-validated set or robust quality metric) and failing when thresholds are not met.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure is at module handoff (API client -> fallback path -> response), not single-function logic. REAL-INFRA: Real network/OS is not required; an in-process client stub returning 503 reproduces the boundary failure. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking does not inherently hide this if it returns realistic 503/error payloads. LLM-DEP: No dependence on model output quality; only error-path orchestration. MULTI-STEP: Not a multi-agentic pipeline. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network needed, (b) no real AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no planner/tool/critic-style LLM handoff. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is specifically "returns a 503 error" and "falls back ... without returning an error," which is control-flow behavior reproducible with in-process stubs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client adapter + fallback translator + response layer; force client to return 503; assert caller gets successful fallback translation and no propagated service error.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Behavior validates repository/translator wiring against actual SQLite reads, not just pure lookup logic. REAL-INFRA: Uses local DB/filesystem only; this fits Integration by definition. MOCK-HIDE: Replacing DB with in-memory map could hide SQL/schema/query bugs. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no external OS/network/subprocess dependency beyond local DB, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-orchestration boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary SQLite DB with known phrase rows, run fallback translator through its real DB access layer, and assert exact stored translation is returned for seeded phrases.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Real failure boundary is algorithm output flowing into persistence (compute + store), not only standalone distance math. REAL-INFRA: No external network/subprocess needed; local storage integration is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking persistence can miss serialization/schema/transaction bugs in metric storage. LLM-DEP: Not dependent on real model judgment; use deterministic strings. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agentic chain. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no external real infra requirement, (b) no AI output quality dependency, (c) no multi-agentic sequence, (d) no LLM-stage orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic primary/fallback text pairs through the quality tracker with real storage wiring; assert computed edit distance values and persisted records match expected results.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure appears in coordination between response selection and asynchronous quality-logging path. REAL-INFRA: Can be tested with in-process async worker/executor and local storage; no real external infra required. MOCK-HIDE: Fully mocking background executor can hide race/order bugs, so use real in-process async components. LLM-DEP: This behavior checks source selection and side-effect logging, not translation quality. MULTI-STEP: Not a multi-agentic decision pipeline. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess, (b) no dependence on model output quality, (c) no chained agentic steps, (d) no LLM-governed multi-stage handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translators enabled, assert immediate response uses primary output, then assert background task eventually persists quality comparison without blocking caller path.
